title: diplotaxis muralis subsp. ceratophylla
intro: Diplotaxis muralis subsp. ceratophylla, also known as Rock Rocket or Wild Rocket, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its slender stems, small white or yellowish flowers, and a peppery flavor. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and stems have been used in herbal medicine due to their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe digestive issues, wounds, and skin irritations. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and providing antioxidant properties, making it a popular remedy for various health conditions. Diplotaxis muralis subsp. ceratophylla can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, salves, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, fever, and respiratory issues.
